title: Stock detail and equity order ticket
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass
cliGuidance: Use this as the strongest browser proof that account_number can preselect stock-detail and buy/sell ticket account context. The order ticket has separate account, order type, unit/dollars/shares, and amount controls; CLI/MCP writes must still use API-first dry-run planners and double live-write gates.

title: Options chain symbol page and builder
observedBy: Sanitized XBI options-chain CDP capture plus 2026-06-02 desktop UI notes
cliGuidance: The browser URL does not encode selected expiration, strike, buy/sell, or call/put. Treat /options/chains/{symbol} as a shell: chain id, expirations, contract ids, prices, Greeks, collateral, and strategy quote state live in API queries and UI state. Use read-only chain/instrument/marketdata calls first, then emit options/orders dry-run bodies only.

title: Per-account investing settings
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass
cliGuidance: Strong account-context propagation, but the surface contains settings that can change margin, DRIP, options eligibility, and account status. Keep read-first and require exact-action approval for any mutation.

title: Stock lending account page
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass plus user-observed account-number preselection on 2026-06-02
cliGuidance: The URL preserves account_number and the user observed it can preselect the account dropdown, but some page-level reads in the capture still used the default selected portfolio context. Prefer direct API routes and explicit account fields over assuming URL propagation.

title: Account hub pages with account dropdown
observedBy: User-observed account-number preselection plus sanitized account-section CDP capture
cliGuidance: Full-scope account pages with dropdowns should be modeled as account-context candidates, but not all sections consume the query the same way. Build URLs for navigation/research; for automation use explicit API account_number or account_numbers parameters.

title: History, reports, statements, and tax pages
cliGuidance: These routes are read-heavy but private. Keep public docs sanitized and prefer API filters over URL-state assumptions.

title: Robinhood Legend active options layout
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass
cliGuidance: Legend is valuable for options endpoint discovery, but the observed account context came from Legend's own account combobox rather than the URL query. Do not rely on account_number in Legend URLs.

title: Transfers, deposits, ACH, and debit-card hub
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass plus full-scope account-number hypothesis from user notes
cliGuidance: Use API-first money-movement planners. The transfer hub did not use the supplied account_number query directly in this pass and contains write/destructive operations. Deposits, withdrawals, debit cards, ACH relationships, and contribution flows should stay exact-approval gated.

title: Recurring investments page
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass
cliGuidance: Page query does not filter the recurring hub globally. Keep using first-class recurring CLI commands with explicit --account filtering and dry-run/live-write gates.

title: Classic home options and stocks right rail
observedBy: Desktop screenshots from 2026-06-02 19:10 local time plus authenticated browser route captures
cliGuidance: The screenshots show the classic right rail exposes options rows, a display menu (Last Price, Your Equity, Today's Return, Total Return), stock rows, and an account dropdown. Treat it as a workflow discovery surface; prefer API calls for automation.

title: Legacy stock options subpath
observedBy: Chrome DevTools MCP authenticated browser pass
cliGuidance: This path returned Robinhood's 404 page in the 2026-06-02 browser pass. Use stock detail and Legend for current options discovery.
